violations.text: The Person in charge did not assure compliance with critical code requirements. Management's plan for assessing, monitoring, and controlling foodborne disease hazards is inadequate. Establishment to maintain temperature log for walk-in cooler. Log to be available for review upon re-inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 04/29/2026

-Soap was not available at the hand-washing sink in the produce area. Hand-washing sinks must be provided with a supply of hand cleaning liquid, powder, or bar soap. -The hand-washing sink in the produce area was lacking paper towels. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/29/2026
Edam cheese was observed moldy to sight. Food was voluntarily disposed of. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/29/2026

Raw animal foods such as raw chorizo were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods such as hot dogs in the reach-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/29/2026

Various foods in walk-in cooler were observed held at 46-50 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Time/temperature abused foods were voluntarily disposed of. See disposal list. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/29/2026

-Residential insecticide was observed in establishment. -An open plate of cheese was observed in the basement for bait. PIC informed that all pest control services to be conducted by a licensed technician. New Violation. Correct By: 04/29/2026

The presence of large rodents is not controlled as evidenced by droppings (old and new) observed in basement area. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Establishment to work in conjunction with licensed pest control company to eliminate pest issue. New Violation. Correct By: 04/29/2026

Single service items are not being stored properly in their original protective package and/or are stored on a surface that is not clean in the basement area. Styrofoam containers were observed in direct contact with rat droppings. Styrofoam containers were voluntarily disposed of. New Violation. Correct By: 04/29/2026
Establishment does not have drain plugs for 3 bay sink. Drain plugs required to effectively wash, rinse and sanitize dishes. New Violation. Correct By: 05/13/2026

The cutting boards in the produce packaging area and meat counter are very scratched and scored. Cutting blocks and boards that are subject to scratching and scoring shall be resurfaced if they can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized, or discarded if they are not capable of being resurfaced. New Violation. Correct By: 05/13/2026

The walk-in cooler was observed at 47-48 degrees Fahrenheit at onset of inspection. Service was called during time of inspection. Technician repaired issues and ambient of walk-in cooler was 39 degrees Fahrenheit. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/29/2026

The non-food contact surfaces of the shelving the basement have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and rodent droppings. All equipment to be thoroughly cleaned and sanitized. New Violation. Correct By: 05/13/2026
The non-food contact surfaces of the meat display cases have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and ice accumulation. New Violation. Correct By: 05/13/2026
The dumpster area was observed with an accumulation of trash and excessive plastic crates. Area to be maintained to prevent harborage conditions for pests. New Violation. Correct By: 05/13/2026
Openings observed outside leading to basement. The physical facilities to be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 05/13/2026

The floors in the basement (especially under shelving) area have an accumulation of soil residue and food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 05/13/2026

The walk-in cooler evaporator (back side) and fan guards have an accumulation of soil residue and dust. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 04/29/2026
Gaskets for 3 door reach-in freezer was observed in poor repair. New Violation. Correct By: 07/17/2025
The establishment does not have a written procedure for cleaning vomit and diarrhea. State guidance provided during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/17/2025

1. Produce scraps were observed in the hand-washing sink. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. 2. Soap was not available at the hand-washing sink in prep area. Hand-washing sinks must be provided with a supply of hand cleaning liquid, powder, or bar soap. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/17/2025

Salted cod was observed held at 56 degrees Fahrenheit. Packaging stated 'keep refrigerated'. PIC stated product was delivered today. Invoice was reviewed. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Salted cod was placed into refrigeration to blast chill. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/17/2025

The door to the outside, located in the back, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 05/01/2025

Cat food and cat litter were observed in basement. Foul odor observed in basement during time of inspection. No live animals were observed in establishment during time of inspection. PIC informed to remove tray of cat litter and cat food during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/17/2025
Partition required in between handsink and food prep sink in prep area. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 05/01/2025
Various foods were observed stored on the floor in the exterior storage pod and in the produce area. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 05/01/2025

The following were observed in poor repair: 1. gaskets for 3 door reach-in freezer 2. light in produce cooler held by duct tape 3. glass door at meat cooler observed with cracks and held by tape 4. frame of meat cooler door observed in poor repair New Violation. Correct By: 07/17/2025

The cutting boards in the are very scratched and scored. Cutting blocks and boards that are subject to scratching and scoring shall be resurfaced if they can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized, or discarded if they are not capable of being resurfaced. New Violation. Correct By: 06/17/2025

The non-food contact surfaces of the following have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris: 1. all meat cooler display cases (exterior and interior) 2. poultry display case (ice accumulation) 3. shelves under prep tables 4. shelves in basement New Violation. Correct By: 05/01/2025
Litter and miscellaneous items were observed surrounding dumpster area. Area to be maintained to prevent harborage conditions for pests. New Violation. Correct By: 05/01/2025

The following areas have an accumulation of soil residue and food debris:1. floors in meat cutting area2. floors in basement3. stairs to basementThe physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 05/01/2025

The floors in the produce walk-in cooler are constructed of unsealed cement which is not durable, easily cleanable, or non-absorbent. The materials for indoor floors must be durable, easily cleanable and nonabsorbent for areas subject to moisture such as food preparation areas, walk-in refrigerators, ware-washing areas, and toilet rooms. New Violation. Correct By: 10/17/2025
Tortilla refrigerator unit, dairy unit, and 2 door salami refrigerator unit door gaskets were observed in poor repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/10/2023

Floors in the walk-in meat unit, walk-in produce unit and floor produce/meat area are in poor repair and constructed of plywood and cement does not seal which is not durable, easily cleanable, or non-absorbent. The materials for indoor floors must be durable, easily cleanable and nonabsorbent for areas subject to moisture such as food preparation areas, walk-in refrigerators, ware-washing areas, and toilet rooms. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/10/2023

Ambient temperature of the walk-in produce/milk unit was observed held at 50 degrees Fahrenheit employee adjusted the unit at the end ambient temperature was 39-40 degrees F. 2 door reach-in/sausage unit was observed at 44 degrees F. All food products were moved to a different unit for active cold hold. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Service must be contact. Units MUST be assets. NO Potentially hazardous food items may be stored in unit until repaired and actively maintaining temperatures of 41 degrees F throughout entire unit. New Violation. Correct By: 08/01/2023

The presence of insects and rodents are not controlled as evidenced by live flies at the retail area and basement and mice droppings at the basement storage area. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Per person in charge pest control is active. By Food inspector new pest control assessment and report is need it by re-inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 08/10/2023
Meat boxes were observed stored on the floor in the meat walk-in cooler. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 08/01/2023
Splash guard is need at the hand sink in the meat department. New Violation. Correct By: 08/01/2023
Large ice condensation was observed at the 2-door reach-in sausage unit, and large chest freezer. New Violation. Correct By: 08/01/2023
Tortilla refrigerator unit, dairy unit, and 2 door salami refrigerator unit door gaskets were observed in poor repair. New Violation. Correct By: 08/15/2023

The non-food contact surfaces of the meat units, 3 door reach-in freezer, walk-in units, meat units have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 08/01/2023

The condensate from the freezer and AC refrigeration unit is draining into a container and at the floor. Condensate drainage and other no sewage liquids must drain into a drain. New Violation. Correct By: 08/10/2023

The floors in the walk-in meat unit, walk-in produce unit and floor produce/meat area are in poor repair and constructed of plywood and cement does not seal which is not durable, easily cleanable, or non-absorbent. The materials for indoor floors must be durable, easily cleanable and nonabsorbent for areas subject to moisture such as food preparation areas, walk-in refrigerators, ware-washing areas, and toilet rooms. New Violation. Correct By: 09/01/2023
Leak was observed at the basement. New Violation. Correct By: 08/10/2023
